paradigm_definition: Construct and execute an explicit model world to explore scenarios, project outcomes, calibrate to evidence, or support decisions when direct experimentation is costly or infeasible.

dimension_views.label: Execution Protocol
dimension_views.definition: How the model world is interrogated or used operationally.

selected_values_expanded.label: Scenario Runs
selected_values_expanded.definition: Run a small, named set of scenarios to compare outcomes and attribute effects to interventions or assumptions.

dimension_views.label: Primary Formalisms
dimension_views.definition: Primary formalism used to represent the modeled world.

selected_values_expanded.label: Lumped-parameter dynamics
selected_values_expanded.definition: few aggregated state variables; no explicit spatial resolution

selected_values_expanded.label: Predict
selected_values_expanded.definition: Use simulation as a forward model to project outcomes under specified inputs and assumptions, emphasizing fidelity and repeatability.

support_edge_hints.operator_family: Coarse grain or surrogate to accelerate simulation
support_edge_hints.mechanism_summary: Figure-2 recurrent interaction pattern: coarse grain or surrogate to accelerate simulation. Use when transformation supports simulation through this recurrent interaction pattern.
Figure-2 recurrent interaction pattern: uncertainty propagation and risk bounding. Use when probabilistic supports simulation through this recurrent interaction pattern.
